title: RDB Loans
summary: Details for RDB Loans.
description:
**1\. Loans and advance (Regional Development Bank)**

The loan schemes offered by RDB are in line with empowering Sri Lankans. An array of small scale loans are on offer to priority sectors such as Agriculture, Fisheries , Small Industries, which are usually not available in many financial institutions.

**_General Conditions_**

  1. Should be a permanent resident in Sri Lanka.
  2. Should be a person completed 18 years of age. If the age is beyond 55 years any other family member could join with for applying.
  3. Should not be a defaulter to any loan obtained from Banks or any other Financial Institution.
  4. Should be a legally competent person.
  5. Respective person should be an account holder at the time of applying the loan.
  6. Should be a person who could repay the money.
  7. For any inquiries, please contact us on our hotline at 011 203 5454



  


**[SME Sector Specialized Loan](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#sme-sector-specialized-loan)**

he country which is rapidly moving forward with a vibrant economy means, it is necessary that due support is rendered to targeted sectors. Which is why RDB has involved itself in micro financing. Pradeshiya Sanwardhana Bank has always been assisting small and medium scale enterprises, such as small scale industrialists, entrepreneurs and self employed individuals. These facilities are available on soft securities at concessional interest rates, convenient repayment periods with reasonable grace period. The facility requests are mostly evaluated on the viability of the project. These loan schemes are operated through refinance facility of Ministry of Finance.

**[SME& Micro Finance Sector](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#sme-micro-finance-sector)**

Pradeshiya Sanwaradhana Bank's main purpose is to facilitate Regional Economic Development of the country by promoting Agriculture, Industry, Trade, Commerce, Livestock, Fisheries activities and Empowerment of women mainly by granting financial assistance through Micro Finance.

**[Loans for Self Employment](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#loans-for-self-employment)**

A number of start up loans are available at RDB under different brand names for different sectors SEPI (Self Employment Promotion Innovative loan scheme) loans are offered to entrepreneurs who have obtained professional qualifications in a specific field and wish to start up a project . These loans ,operated under refinance facility of Central Bank of Sri Lanka, are provided at attractive interest rates on soft securities.

**[Saubhagya](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#saubhagya)**

Saubhagya Loans are offered with the main aim of boosting self employment in the country. Competitive interest rates are offered exclusively to encourage the self employment sector.

**[Agriculture](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#agriculture)**

RDB is one of the few financial institutions in the country that provides loans to farmers for paddy and soya bean cultivation and to stock their produce until the market prices become favorable in a short time span of 270 days. RDB was amongst the pioneers who assisted soya bean farmers to stock soya beans when the prices drastically dropped due to excessive production. These loans too are offered at concessional interest rates against soft securities.

**[Fisheries Industry Loans](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#fisheries-industry-loans)**

Loans to the fisheries sector are available at concessionary rates and soft securities to purchase fishing equipment and for community development. Loan facilities are also offered to purchase multi-day fishing boats and other small boats. RDB also offers special loans for Inland fish farming and Ornamental fish breeding.

**[Techno Based Industries](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#techno-based-industries)**

RDB is aware of the ongoing fuel crisis. Addressing the scarcity issue, RDB offers loans for solar power generation and small scale power generation projects at comfortable terms and at lower interest rates.

**[Commercial& Business Purpose Loans](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#commercial-business-purpose-loans)**

RDBs commercial and business loans are offered for retail trading and stock purchasing purposes. The uniqueness of these facilities are, that most of these facilities are operated through the banks' collection agents who make visits from door to door. Thus, the clientele has the comfort of servicing the debts even without coming to the bank.

**[Transport Sector Loans](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#transport-sector-loans)**

RDB is also among the few banks that provide loans to meet transport requirements of rural sector providing loans to purchase vehicles. These loan facilities are available at low interest rates and soft securities.

**[Isuru](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#isuru)**

Isuru is a loan scheme designed with the aim of eliminating poverty. Small groups comprising of five to seven women earning incomes lower than the poverty level could apply for these loans for income generating projects. These facilities are also offered at concessionary interest rates and on soft securities.

**[Housing Loans](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#housing-loans)**

RDB offers special housing loan schemes for the lower income sectors at attractive interest rates. These loans are granted with repayment periods up to 15 years. Housing loans are offered for purposes such as outright purchase of a house, to buy a land to build a house, renovations and to add new units to an existing house.

  


**[Ranaviru Divi naguma](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/component/info/?id=2368&catid=25&task=info&lang=en#ranaviru-divi-naguma)**

RDB launched the Ranaviru Divi Neguma program with the objective of integrating the family members of war heroes and disabled war heroes into the countrys' development process and uplift their living standards. The Government allocated Rs. 400 million for this scheme through the 2012 Budget. The families of war heroes and disabled soldiers who like to initiate their own projects are given loans not exceeding Rs. 500,000 under this program through the Pradeshiya Sanwaradhana Bank. This program has been launched by the bank along with the Economic Development Ministry and Ranaviru Seva Authority.

  


#### **Organization Information**

Regional Development Bank

**The Premier State Owned Development Bank in Sri Lanka.**

Established under the Pradheshiya Sanwardana Bank Act No.41 of 2008.

Head Office,

No. 933,

Kandy Road, Wedamulla,

Kelaniya (11600)

Sri Lanka.

Fax Nos:+94 11 299 1328

Email: pro@rdb.lk

Website: [www.rdb.lk](http://www.rdb.lk/)

title: Loan Facilites for Journalists
summary: Details for Loan Facilites for Journalists.
description:
**Loan Facilities for Journalists**

As stipulated in the “Mahinda Chinthanaya” Development Programme, the Ministry of Mass Media and Information wishes to grant concessionary credit facilities to local journalists to purchase computers, Motor bicycles and Media equipment through People’s Bank.

Under this Program;

  1. Motor Bicycles
  2. Computers
  3. Cameras
  4. Fax Machines



**Objective**

The objective of this project is help enhance the professional aptitudes and skills of the journalists who have been rendering a valuable service towards development of the Government Information.

**Target Group**

The Journalists/Media personnel who have been accredited by the Department of the Government Information.

**Items and Loan Limits**

  
**Item**| **Loan Limits(Rs)**| **Maximum Repayment Period**  
---|---|---  
Motor Bicycles| 100,000.00| 3 Years  
Computers| 100,000.00| 3 Years  
Cameras| 50,000.00| 2 Years  
‍Fax| 15,000.00| 2 Years  
  
**Who can apply ?**

  1. Journalists who have a service of not less than three years in state or private sector media Institution as journalists.
  2. Freelance journalists with not less than five years of service in the media field.
  3. Should be between 18-60 years of age.
  4. Should have a permanent source of income.
  5. Annual income should not be less than Rs. 144,000.00



(relevant documents should be submitted in support of the veracity of the above facts)

**Application Procedure**

Application should be made on the following format. Duly completed applications should be sent under registered post clearly indicating the expected item on the top left-hand corner of the envelope enclosing the application to receive on or before ………..

**Mode of Selection**

The eligible loan recipients will be selected by a selection committee appointed by the Secretary of the Ministry Mass Media and Information. As far as possible priority will be given to the applicants from difficult areas. In this case, the decision of the secretay shall be final and conclusive.

**Specimen[Application Form](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/)**

title: Personal Loans
summary: Details for Personal Loans.
description:
**Personal Loans**

  


This loan scheme provides the facility to public & private sector permanent employees to obtain loans within short period for daily / personal requirements under minimum collateral up to the maximum loan amount of Rs.250,000/- (Loan application will be released as per the requirement of loan client after the discussion with the manager)

  


  


**Eligibility of the Borrower**

  1. Applicant should be permanent employee of the public or private institution and age over 18 years and below 55 years and should have capacity to enter into an agreement with the bank.
  2.   3. Possess the National Identity Card
  4.   5. Should not be a defaulter asa Borrower / Guarantor – The CRIB Status should be clear.



**Quantum of loan**

Maximum limit is Rs.250,000/-

  


**Rate of Interest**

The rate of interest is 17% p.a. A concessionary rate of 13% p.a. with a rebate of 4% is applied for repayment on due date.

  


**Repayment period**

Maximum repayment period of the loan is 3 years.

  


**Mode of repayment**

The borrower should agree to settle the loan and interest in equal monthly installments. Further, he should agree to open savings account in the bank and deposit required funds to the said account to settle the loan capital and interest. In addition to this account, the Bank may request to open a build up / compulsory savings accounts where withdrawals are restricted.

  


**Securities**

  1. Guarantee of two persons acceptable to the bank.To obtain guarantors who have permanent employment( Government or Private)
  2. Security over movable assets (Eg. Vehicle/ Machinery etc.) if necessary,
  3. Other acceptable securities (Eg. Life insurance policy / Share certificate)

**Eligibility**

  2. Should not be a defaulter of any financial institutions

**Securities**

  1. Guarantee of two persons or any other security acceptable to the Bank.
  2. Mortgage over project assets.



**Documents Required**

  1. [Loan Application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/)
  2. Guarantors' Statements
  3. Project Report recommended by Aquatic Resource Development and Quality improvement Project



** _Fisheries Credit Scheme (Without subsidy support)_**

To purchase equipment.

  


**Eligibility**

  1. Be an Account holder of the Bank.
  2. Should not be a defaulter of the Bank or an institutions.
  3. Be able to bring 25% of the project cost.



**Security**

  1. Mortgage over fishing craft, engine and gears
  2. Assignment over comprehensive Insurance cover or
  3. Any other security acceptable to the bank



**Documents Required**

description:
**Obtaining a Taxpayer Identification Number(TIN)**

**Eligibility**

Those who are liable to pay Income Tax, Value Added Tax (VAT), Nation Building Tax (NBT), Economic Service Charges (ESC). Pay As You Earn tax (PAYE) and Stamp Duty or should be Importer/Exporter.

**Places from where Applications can be Obtained**

  1. Taxpayer Service Unit (TSU)_(1_ _st_ _Floor, South Wing, Department of Inland Revenue, Chittampalam A. Gardiner Mawatha, Colombo 02.)_
  2. IRD Metropolitan or Regional Offices Island wide.
  3. IRD Web Portal _[(via: e-Services)](https://www.ird.gov.lk/en/Downloads/SitePages/Forms.aspx?menuid=1603)_



**Applications**

  1. Application Form for Taxpayer Registration (For Company)
  2. Application Form for Taxpayer Registration (For Individual and Proprietorship)
  3. Application Form for Taxpayer Registration (For Partnership / Joint Venture / Project)
  4. Application Form for Taxpayer Registration (For NGO / Charity / Co-op /Associations & Clubs / Semi Government Institutions / Government Institutions / Provident Fund / Trust / Embassy / High Commissions / Others)



**Process of Submitting Applications**

  1. Taxpayer Service Unit (TSU)_(1_ _st_ _Floor, South Wing, Department of Inland Revenue, Chittampalam A. Gardiner Mawatha, Colombo 02.)_

title: GUIDELINES AND CONDITIONS APPLICABLE FOR THE ISSUE OF EXCISE LICENSES
summary: Details for GUIDELINES AND CONDITIONS APPLICABLE FOR THE ISSUE OF EXCISE LICENSES.
description:
**GUIDELINES AND CONDITIONS APPLICABLE FOR THE ISSUE OF EXCISE LICENSES**

******1.**| **(a)**| **The present holders of licences will not be entitled to automatic renewal thereof. On termination of the validity of a licence, if Licensee, so desires to renew his licence, an application should be forwarded at least fifteen (15) days prior to the expiry of such licence.********Applications will be considered by the Commissioner General of Excise in terms of these guidelines and conditions, and subject to any other directions as stipulated in the Excise Ordinance.******  
---|---|---  
**2.****************3.********************4.******| ****| **The application forms (as set out in Schedule II) must be obtained either from the Excise Head Office or from any Assistant Excise Commissioner's Office situated island wide, on payment of the relevant application fee. However, the issue of an application form to the applicant will not guarantee the grant of a license to such person.********Applications must be completed in all respects and all required documents annexed. Incomplete application or applications submitted without the necessary documents and reports or which does not conform to the guidelines and conditions will be rejected.********Completed applications should be addressed to the Assistant Excise Commissioner and forwarded under registered cover to the Office of the Assistant Excise Commissioner of the Province where the premises for which application is made on the prescribed form (as set out in Schedule II)******  
**5.**| ****| **When applying for a new licence or applying for renewal of an existing licence, the applicant should pay a non-refundable application fee of Rs. 7,500.00.******  
**6.**| ****| **A licence will be issued for a period of three consecutive financial years, except in the case of licences issued to Tourist Board approved Restaurants / Tourist Board approved Hotels, where the validity of such licences will be for one year only. In the case of fresh licence being issued during a financial year, such licences will be issued for the remaining period of that year and for two further consecutive financial years except in the case of licences issued to Tourist Board approved Restaurants/Tourist Board approved Hotels, where such licences will be issued for the remaining period of that year only.******  
**7.************************************************************8.**| ****| **All licences issued to successful applicants under the Excise Ordinance shall be subject to a licence fee of such amount as is specified in Schedule I to these rules. Licence fees will be charged on a _pro rata_ basis in the case of new licences initially issued for part of a year. Though the licence is issued for 3 years, the licence fee could be paid annually. Payment should be made at least seven working days prior to the commencement of the relevant financial year. However, in the event the fee is not received by the relevant Divisional Secretary at least seven working days after the commencement of a financial year, an additional four weeks will be allowed to make payment subject to an additional payment of a penalty of 1% of the value of the licence fee of such delay. After that the penalty will be 3% of the value of the license fee. A licence holder shall not be allowed to carry on business in the licensed premises until the licence fee is paid for the relevant financial year. ********In addition to the licence fee, a security deposit is payable on each type of licence as set out in Column II of Schedule I hereof, at rates applicable in respect of each category of license. Every person to whom a licence is issued under the Ordinance shall execute a Counterpart Agreement to cover the tenure of the licence. The security deposit shall be required for the due performance of the Counterpart Agreement.******  
**9.**| ****| **Where the Commissioner General of Excise refuses an application for the issue of a liquor licence, he should convey his decision by registered post to the applicant within 30 days from the date he received the application stating his reasons for such refusal, and also inform the applicant to show cause if any justifying the granting of a licence. Accordingly the applicant may show cause as to why his application should not be refused by the Commissioner General of Excise by registered post, within two weeks from the date he receives such communication from the Commissioner General of Excise in writing with supporting documents if any, addressed to the Commissioner General of Excise.******  
**10.**| ****| **The Commissioner General of Excise shall thereafter and not later than 30 days from the date he receives the applicant's reply, having considered all relevant materials and after such inquiries, he may deem expedient, decide whether the applicant should be issued a liquor licence, or affirm the refusal of such licence. The said decision should be forthwith communicated in writing to the applicant by registered post.******  
**11.**| ****| **In determining the number of licences to be issued in respect of each Province, in respect of the following categories of licenses, consideration will be given to the density of the population, patterns of consumption of liquor and other social requirements of that area. The number of licences to be currently issued in respect of each Province for the following categories of Licences are as follows: -******  
  
****

  
**Province**| **No. of Retail Licences (FL/4)**| **No. of Hotel Licenses (FL/7) (without the approval of the Tourist Board)**| **No. of Restaurant Licenses (FL/11) (without the approval of the Tourist Board)******  
---|---|---|---  
**Western**| **312**| **8**| **146**  
**Southern**| **104**| **-**| **22**  
**Northern**| **54**| **3**| **21**  
**Eastern**| **61**| **7**| **16**  
**Central**| **223**| **8**| **49**  
**North Central**| **65**| **4**| **16**  
**North Western**| **106**| **-**| **39**  
**Uva**| **76**| **4**| **24**  
**Sabaragamuwa**| **103**| **-**| **29**  
**Total**| **1,104**| **34**| **362**  
  
****

**Note: The limitation mentioned in the above paragraph will not apply in respect of retail licenses (FL/04) issued to Super Markets, Grocery Stores and like enterprises where the annual turnover of the applicant company / business or the parent company / business of the applicant exceeds Rupees Seventy Two million per year. (Rs. 72 million)**

****

  
**12.**| **The applicants (new and existing) are required to submit the following documents along with their applications. However, the Commissioner General of Excise shall have the right to refer and extract any information pertaining to any applicant from any file available with the Department of Excise or to call upon the applicant to furnish any information required for the consideration of the issue of licence applied for including particulars of income tax and other taxes payable by the applicant.******  
---|---  
**13.**| ****| **The documents and reports required to be submitted with regard to new applicants:******  
****| ****| **(a)**| **Photocopy of the ground plan of the proposed building (with particulars of floor area) certified by the Local Authority.******  
****| ****| **(b)**| **Proof of right to carry on business at the identified premises by submitting either the original deeds as proof of ownership along with a certified copy of the same which shall be retained in the file maintained for the purpose, or if the premises are not owned by the applicant, a document expressing the consent of the owner of the premises that the proposed business can be carried out undisturbed in the identified premises. Such document should be appropriately certified by a Notary Public. If the proposed premises is mortgaged a letter should be submitted from the relevant financial institution, confirming that the property is under mortgage.******  
****| ****| **(c)**| **Report including the recommendations of the Officer-in-Charge of the Excise Station, Superintendent of Excise and Assistant Commissioner of Excise. (As per Schedule II).******  
****| ****| **(d)****************(e)****************(f)**| **A Declaration by way of an affidavit of the applicant that the applicant is not convicted of any offence under the Penal Code or any other offence involving moral turpitude during the preceding 5 years.********If any complaints are received to the contrary, the Commissioner General of Excise shall upon an inquiry as set out in paragraph 21 hereof refuse to issue a licence/cancel the licence.********A report from the Officer-in-Charge of the Police Station where the licensed premises is situated that the applicant is not convicted of any offence under the Penal Code or the Excise Ordinance during the preceding five (05) years and a Report from the Divisional Secretary where the licensed premises is situated that the applicant is fit and proper person to hold the said licence and there is no objection from the public to the issue of the licence.******  
****| ****| **(g)****************(h)**| **Survey Plan from a Licensed Survey indicating the distance (as the crow flies from boundary to boundary) from the proposed building to the nearest places of public religious worship and to schools.********If any complaints are received of a violation of the prescribed distance to schools/religious places from the licence premises, a report from the Government Superintendent of Surveys will be called to clarify and ascertain the actual distance. The cost of such survey shall be borne by the licensee. Upon such survey if it is found that the distance rule has been violated the licence will be cancelled.******  
****| ****| **(i)**| **A Business Registration Certificate where applicable in terms of the Business Names Act No. 7 of 1987 and a Certificate of Incorporation in respect of an application by a Company certified as a true copy by a Notary Public.******  
****| ****| **(j)**| **Proof of payment (non refundable application fee).******  
**14.**| ****| ****| **With regard to retail liquor licences issued to Enterprises such as Super Markets/Grocery Stores and like enterprises proof of turnover should be supported by either a report from a recognized firm of auditors or from the Department of Inland Revenue confirming that the turnover of the applicant or the parent business/company of the applicant exceeded Rs. 72 million for the previous year.******  
**15.**| ****| ****| **With regard to applications for Club licences, applicants should submit the following reports in addition to the above-mentioned documents and reports.******  
****| ****| ****| **(a)**| **A Certificate of Registration under the Club Act, 17 of 1975.******  
****| ****| ****| **(b)**| **Documents to prove the continued existence of the Club for five (5) years immediately preceding the date of application. (This regulation is not applicable to current holders of licences.)******  
****| ****| ****| **(c)**| **Proof of Operating a Bank Account.******  
****| ****| ****| **(d)**| **A Certificate of Registration from the Ministry of Sports or Ministry of Social Services.******  
**16.****************************17.**| ****| ****| **Proof of payment of Income Tax, Value Added Tax (where applicable) and other relevant taxes on an yearly basis (during the validity of the licence) in respect of the four quarters including monthly payments where applicable immediately preceding 31****st****December of such year, along with the payment of the annual licence fee as set out in Column II of Schedule 1.hereof.********In the event of a failure by a holder of a licence to forward such information, the Commissioner General of Excise shall with the concurrence of the Secretary to the Treasury cancel such licence.******  
**18.**| ****| ****| **New applicants applying for licences for the sale of Beer, Ale, Stout and Wines (FL 22A) are required to submit the documents and reports as referred to under sub paragraphs (a), (b), (c), (d), (f), (g), (i) and (j) of paragraph 13.******  
**19.**| ****| **The documents and reports necessary to be submitted for existing licence****holders (for all categories):******  
****| ****| **(a)**| **Documents and reports referred to in sub paragraphs (b), (c), (d) and (j) of paragraph 13.******  
****| ****| **(b)**| **A Report from the Assistant Commissioner of Excise on the detections if any made in the premises during the 03 years immediately preceding the year for which application is made.**  
  
****

**20\. Requirements regarding location and description of type of premises:**

****

**(a) Outlets for the sale of liquor off the premises should have a minimum floor area of 250 Sq.ft. Premises selling liquor for consumption on the premises should have a minimum floor area of 500 Sq.ft.**

****

**This requirement will not apply to licences for the retail sale of Beer, Ale, Stout and Wines (FL 22A).**

****

**(b) Applicants for the non Tourist Board type FL07 or FL08 Hotel licences and FL11 Restaurant Licences will comply with the additional requirements regarding location and premises as enumerated in Schedule IV. Hotel as distinct from Restaurant Licences will be considered in respect of premises having at least five rooms for the exclusive use of visitors.**

****

**(c) The location of premises for operation of licences in respect of sale of liquor off the premises should be 100 meters away (as the crow flies from boundary to boundary) from Schools and places of public religious worship and in respect of licences for selling liquor for consumption within the premises,**

description:
**Obtain of Postal Identity Card**

**Eligibility** : Sri Lankan citizenship

**Process of Submitting** : In relation to complaints only.

**Places From Where[Application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/) Forms can be Obtained**

  1. General Post Office, Bristol Street, Colombo 01.
  2. All Post Offices and Sub Post Offices in the island.



**Fees Payable for Obtaining[Application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/) Form: **No fee is charged

**Hours for Submission of[Application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/)s**: From 8:30 am to 3:30 pm

**Fees for Providing the Service** : Stamps for Rs. 50.00

**Time Taken to Provide the Service**(Ordinary Service and Priority Service): Within one day on submission to the general Post Office.

**Supporting Documents Required** :

  1. [Application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/)s
  2. [Birth Certificate](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/index.php/en/component/info/?id=367&task=info&back=0&pid=722)

Senior Administrative Secretary(Head-Quarters)| I.L.L| Post Head- Quarters| -| -| -  
  
**Exceptions or Instances Outside the Above Requirements and Special Information**

Two copies of 3 x 2 size photograph of the bust only which should be clear and in the posture of looking front should be forwarded. One copy of the photograph should be posted in the prescribed place in the application and the applicant should place his signature so as to appear one half on the application and the other half on the photograph in the presence of Post Master and two witnesses. The signature should also be placed on the other photograph so as not to appear any post on the face of the photograph in front of the Post Master.

summary: Details for Obtain a Certified Copy of the Certificate of Birth.
description:
**Required Forms** [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Application_for_birth_certificate_or_search_registeres.pdf)

**Obtain a Certified Copy of the Certificate of Birth**

  1. Refer the [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Application_for_birth_certificate_or_search_registeres.pdf) to the Divisional Secretariat relevant to the area where the birth occurred.
  2. Application can obtain from any Divisional Secretariat.
  3. Fees chargeable
  4. Where the date of registration or the no. of the entry is given the fee for one copy of the certificate is Rs. 100.00
  5. Where the date of registration or the no. of the entry is not given and a search of registers not exceeding two years is involved fee for one copy of the certificate is Rs. 200.00
  6. Required fee should pay to the Divisional Secretariat.
  7. Duly filled [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Application_for_birth_certificate_or_search_registeres.pdf) should submit to the Divisional Secretariat.
  8. If you like to obtain the certificate of birth by post, submit a stamped envelop with the [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Application_for_birth_certificate_or_search_registeres.pdf).
  9. If the birth is registered a birth certificate will be send and if the birth is not registered a letter to the effect will be send.

title: Alteration of Information in a Birth Register
summary: Details for Alteration of Information in a Birth Register.
description:
**Required Forms** [declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b37a.pdf)

**Alteration of Information in a Birth Register**

  1. In a Birth certificate,
  2. Information in cage 01 (Date & place of birth)
  3. Information in cage 03 (Sex)
  4. Information in cage 05 (Information of mother - if mother's name has been changed after the registration of the birth, it is not possible under this provision)
  5. Information in cage 09 (Informant's information) may be altered
  6. Produce the [declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b37a.pdf) to the Additional District Registrar of the Divisional Secretariat of the area where birth occurred.
  7. The [declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b37a.pdf) should be produced by
  8. Owner of the birth certificate
  9. Father or Mother
  10. Legal guardian
  11. A person who is dissatisfied with any of the information appearing in the birth register
  12. Fee chargeable is Rs. 50.00
  13. A certified copy of the birth register to be amended should be essentially attached to the [declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b37a.pdf).
  14. Following are some written evidences which may be produced to substantiate the request made in the [declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b37a.pdf),
  15. Parents' marriage certificate
  16. List of children/ brothers and sisters
  17. Birth certificate of father/ mother
  18. Birth certificate of a child born after the registration of parents' marriage
  19. In case there is no child born after the registration of the marriage of parents, birth certificate of a elder or younger child of the subject
  20. Certified copies of records available in hospitals, maternity homes or midwife's records
  21. Student's record sheet, extract of the school admission register
  22. Copy of the electoral register where names of the subject and parents are included
  23. If father is deceased, his death certificate

title: Make changes in the present birth certificate through divisional secretariat
summary: Details for Make changes in the present birth certificate through divisional secretariat.
description:
**Obtain a copy of birth certificate from divisional secretariat**

Birth Certificate Copies

  


Timeline

Process Time

  


A new birth certificate can be prepared and obtained by the applicant within 5 to 10 minutes

  


If the applicant provides the number and date stamped on the birth certificate, it will be easier to find the stamp)

  


Note:_

For example, it will take more time to search for missing information

than to obtain a regular certificate.

  


Submission Time

  


Step 1 Obtaining the Application

Applications can be obtained from the inquiry counter of the Divisional Secretariat during office hours.

  


Working Days / Hours

  


Working Days Monday to Friday (Monday and Wednesday are suitable) Opening Hours 9.00 AM - 3.00 PM

  


Step 2 Submitting the Application

  


There is no time requirement if the application is submitted in person. If the application is sent by post, the time it takes to be received by the relevant officer

  


Working days / hours

  


Monday _ to Friday (Monday and Wednesday)

  


Opening hours _ 9.00 am to 12.30 pm 1.00 pm _ 3.00 pm

  


Holidays _ All government and commercial holidays

  


Responsibility for the service

Name of the post division

Birth, Marriage, Death Certificate Registrar Birth, Marriage and Death Certificate Registration Division

  


Applications

Job description

Application form for a birth certificate or search form (E 63) A copy of a valid birth certificate

  


Additional documents required

Not required.

  


Procedure for Submission

1 Obtaining a valid application form Applicant should obtain an application form from any Divisional Secretariat Office Applicant should complete and submit the application form Note:_If there is a situation where the birth certificate cannot be used, it should also be attached. Application Number Place of Birth/Year/Month Form No. B 63

2 An application form should be submitted by post or by hand. The application form should be submitted by hand to the Registrar or mailed to the Registrar along with a stamped addressed envelope

3 Note:_ If the application is sent by post, it should be sent by registered post. It will help the applicant to get the copy quickly if the applicant knows the regular date and number. The birth certificate will be sent to the applicant by post. Application period: 9.00 am to 3.00 pm

  


Special occasions

Special occasions for this service

Step by step procedure (obtaining a copy of the birth certificate)

Step 1 The applicant can obtain a valid application form from the inquiry counter of the Divisional Secretariat.

  


Step 2 Complete and submit the application form

  


Step 3 The application should be submitted by post or personally to the Registrar of the Divisional Secretariat.

  


Note:_ Send the application by post / submit a stamped envelope

  


Step 5 Check the data provided by the Divisional Secretariat and confirm it

  


Step 6 Arrange for the applicant to receive the birth certificate by post

Note:_ If the applicant's application form is cancelled, arrange for the application form to be received by post, informing the reasons for the cancellation.

  


Eligibility

A copy of a birth certificate can be obtained if the person was born in Sri Lanka and was born within the Divisional Secretariat limits.

  


If the birth occurred outside the Divisional Secretariat limits, the Divisional Secretary of the place of birth should be contacted and certified.

  


Note:

The applicant can obtain 3 copies by submitting an application form.

  


Fees for services

A stamp fee must be paid to obtain a birth certificate or/and to get it registered.

  


• A stamp fee of Rs. 25/= must be paid along with the registration number to obtain a copy of the birth certificate.

  


• If the date of registration or date of entry is not known, a certified copy of the documents of 3 months can be obtained for Rs. 50.00

  


• For a period of less than 2 years, Rs. 100.00 per copy

title: Copy of birth certificate from divisional secretariat
summary: Details for Copy of birth certificate from divisional secretariat.
description:
**Obtain of new birth certificate from Divisional Secretariat**

Issuance of a new birth certificate

  


Application forms

  


Application number / name details

  


Application for birth certificate and or registration search _ from B63

  


To obtain a new birth certificate or copy

  


3 months after birth _ from B6

  


Registered birth 3 months after

  


Application for birth certificate and or registration search

  


3 months after birth

  


Additional documents required

  


Not applicable for general cases

  


If supporting documents could not be submitted for more than 3 months, the following documents are required for the birth.

  


•Request letter

  


•Birth certificate issued by the Grama Niladhari

  


•Parents' marriage certificates

  


•Birth certificates of the child's siblings (if any)

  


•A certified letter from the place of birth

  


•Immunization card

  


•Family Health Officer's report

  


•Midwife's report (if available)

  


•Child's horoscope

  


•Child's school report if the child is enrolled in school

  


Processing time

  


Note:

  


If for any reason the required information (completed booklet certified by the hospital) is not received, it may take up to a month to issue the birth certificate

  


Submission time

  


Step 1 Obtaining the application

  


Application forms can be obtained from the Enquiry Counter of the Divisional Secretariat during office hours.

  


Working Days / Hours

  


Working Days Monday to Friday

  


(Monday and Wednesday are suitable) Window Opening Hours

  


9.00 am to 3.00 pm

  


Working Days / Hours

  


Monday to Friday (Monday and Wednesday) Window Opening Hours 9.00 am to 3.00 pm

  


Holidays _ All Government and Commercial Holidays

  


Step by Step Procedure (Registering a New Birth.)

  


Step 1 Applicant obtains a valid application form from the Enquiry Counter or from the Registrar at the Divisional Secretariat.

  


Step 2 Applicant fills and prepares the application form

  


Step 3 Prepares supporting documents

  


Step 4 Mails the application form to the Divisional Secretary or takes it to the Registrar of the Divisional Secretariat Office and delivers it

  


Note:_ The application form is mailed or delivered by hand with a stamped envelope

  


Step 5 The applicant will be made to receive the birth certificate by post.

  


Note:

  


If the application form is cancelled, the application form will be sent to the applicant by post with the reasons for the cancellation.

  


Qualifications

  


If a Sri Lankan was born within the relevant divisional limits, the birth certificate can be legally obtained from the Secretary of the Divisional Secretariat Office.

  


Note:

  


• This information must be provided by the child's mother or father

  


• If the parents are deceased or sick, it must be entered by another guardian

  


• Failure to provide information is a punishable offense.

  


Sample application form with sample data

  


Application form for obtaining a birth certificate

  


3 months after birth

  


Service fees

  


Stamp fees must be paid to obtain a birth certificate or / for registration

  


A stamp fee of Rs. 1/= must be paid for issuing a birth certificate after 3 months and before 1 year.

  


If the certificate is requested more than a year late, a stamp fee of Rs. 5/= must be paid.

title: Adoption of a Child
summary: Details for Adoption of a Child.
description:
**Adoption of a Child**

  1. Age of the adoptee child should not be more than 14 years.
  2. Requirements to be met by the applicant
  3. He / She should be more than 25 years of age.
  4. The age gap between the applicant and the adoptee child should be at least 21 years.
  5. This will not apply if the child concerned is a descendant of the applicant / his brother or sister / any of their married partners.
  6. Child's consent is required if he / she is over 10 years old
  7. Spouses (husband and wife) should produce an application
  8. Order of adoption is issued by the District Court
  9. This order should be registered at the Registrar General's Department
  10. Birth of the adoption child can be re-registered
  11. Following documents should be submitted for re-registration of such birth
  12. Application for a re-registration of a birth
  13. Parents' certificate of Marriage
  14. Parents' certificates Births
  15. Certificate of the Adoption
  16. The application for re-registration of a birth should be produced to the Zonal Office of the Registrar General's Department to which the area where the child is born belongs to.

summary: Details for Registration of a Birth Occurred at a Foreign Country.
description:
**Required Forms** [Declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Particulars_for_registration_of_a_birth.pdf)

**Registration of a Birth Occurred at a Foreign Country**

  1. Births of children born to Sri Lankan parents in foreign countries can be registered.
  2. Birth should be registered at the Ambassador/ High Commission of Sri Lanka in the country where birth occurred.
  3. Appropriate persons for informing a birth for registration,
  4. Father
  5. Mother
  6. Guardian
  7. [Declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Particulars_for_registration_of_a_birth.pdf) to inform the birth can obtained from the Ambassador's / Commissioner's Office of Sri Lanka.
  8. Documents to be submitted
  9. Duly perfected declaration
  10. Relevant documents in proof of the birth
  11. A birth can registered free of charge within a period of 03 months.
  12. A copy of the birth certificate is issued to the informant free of charge.
  13. Births of persons occurred in a foreign country and not registered there and presently have arrived in Sri Lanka can be registered.
  14. Further information on this regard can be obtained from the Assistant Registrar General of the Central Record Room of the Registrar General's Department. Telephone: +94 112329773 or +94 112433075

**Registration of a Birth Occurred at Home**

  1. Inform the Grama Niladari within 07 days of the birth is occurred.
  2. Grama Niladari shall send a report to the registrar of the area.
  3. The birth is registered by the registrar of births and deaths of the area where birth has occurred.
  4. Duly filled [declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Particulars_for_registration_of_a_birth.pdf) of birth shall submit to the registrar of births and deaths of the area. Declaration of birth can be obtained from the registrar.
  5. Persons required to give information,
  6. Father
  7. Mother
  8. Each person present at the birth
  9. Person have in charge of the child
  10. Birth may be register free of charge within a period of 03 months. Birth can be registered after 03 months. Please see the delayed registration of births.
  11. Certificate of birth is issued to the informant free of charge.

  1. If a birth has not been registered with 03 months since its occurrence it may be registered.
  2. [Declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b6.pdf) should be produced to the Additional District Registrar of the Divisional Secretariat where birth occurred.
  3. Persons eligible for this
  4. Any party having interest of
  5. One of the parents
  6. Fee chargeable is Rs. 50.00
  7. Only the following documents should be submitted with the declarations produced within one year since the occurrences of the birth.
  8. Marriage certificate of parents if they are married
  9. One of following documents in proof of the date and place of birth of the subject.
  10. Hospital birth report or certified copy
  11. Grama Niladhari birth report or certified copy
  12. Estate birth report or certified copy
  13. Public Health Officer's certificate
  14. Following documents should be produced with the declaration produced after one year since occurrence of the birth.
  15. Marriage certificate of parents if they are married
  16. Nil result sheet
  17. One of the following documents indicating date and place of birth of the person
  18. Hospital report of certified copy
  19. Grama Niladhari report or certified copy
  20. Estate birth report or certified copy
  21. Copy of the Midwife's birth register
  22. In case of non-possession of any of the above documents,
  23. Health development record
  24. Certificate of baptism
  25. Certified copy of the school admission register / pupil's record sheet
  26. Estate welfare officer's report
  27. Horoscope of the record of time prepared on a proximate date to the day on which the birth occurred
  28. If any of these documents are not available, declaration including details having knowledge of and an affidavit.

description:

**Obtain a Certified Copy of the Certificate of Marriage**

  1. Refer the [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Application_for_certificate_of_marriage.pdf) to the Divisional Secretariat of the area where the registration took place.
  2. Application can obtain from any Divisional Secretariat.
  3. Fees chargeable
  4. Where the date of registration or the no. of the entry is given the fee for one copy of the certificate is Rs.100.00
  5. Where the date of registration or the no. of the entry is not given and a search of registers not exceeding two years is involved fee for one copy of the certificate is Rs.200.00
  6. Required fee should submit to the Divisional Secretariat.
  7. Duly filled [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Application_for_certificate_of_marriage.pdf) should be submit to the Divisional Secretariat.
  8. If you like to obtain the certificate of marriage by post, submit a stamped envelop with the [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Application_for_certificate_of_marriage.pdf).
  9. If the marriage is registered, a certificate of marriage will be send and if the marriage is not registered a letter to the effect will be send.

title: Registration of Marriages (General)
summary: Details for Registration of Marriages (General).
description:
**Required Forms** _[Marriage notice](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b100.pdf)_

**Registration of Marriages (General)**

  1. Everyone other than where both parties are Muslims, can register their marriage under Marriage(general) Registration Ordinance.
  2. [Marriage notice](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b100.pdf) should be written and attested in duplicate and handed over to the Registrar of marriages of the division
  3. Persons authorized to attest marriage notice;
  4. Registrar of marriages of the division
  5. Justice of peace
  6. Notary public
  7. A Minister
  8. Requirements for submitting a marriage notice:
  9. Requirement of residence of the parties in the relevant division.
  10. Residence of parties in the relevant division within 10 days preceding submission of the marriage notice.
  11. Residence of parties in different divisions within 10 days preceding submission of the marriage notice.
  12. If one party was not living in Sri Lanka within 10 days preceding submission of the marriage notice, residence of the other party in Sri Lanka for such 10 days.
  13. If none of the parties had not been living in Sri Lanka within 10 days preceding submission of the marriage notice, residence of one party for 04 days in Sri Lanka.
  14. Completion of age of 18 yrs. by both parties as at their previous birth day.
  15. The parties should not have any kind of relationship prohibited in law.
  16. No party should have entered into any legal marriage which is valid at the time.
  17. 14 days should be lapsed since submission of marriage notice for registration of a marriage(general). Possibility prevails to register general marriage before lapse of such 14 days.
  18. Request may be made in order to register a marriage outside the office.
  19. Fees chargeable by the Registrar for registration of a marriage is as follows.



  


  
No.| Duty| Payable to Whom| Payable by Whom| Amount| Payment Type  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
1| Entering a notice of marriage at Registrar’s office or at any other place| Registrar| Applicant| 100.00| Cash  
2| Entering a notice of marriage at Additional District Registrar's Office or District Registrar's office or at any other place| Additional District Registrar or District Registrar| Applicant| 100.00| Cash  
3| Issuing Registrar's certificate on a notice of marriage| Registrar| Parties to marriage| 100.00| Cash  
4| Issuing Registrar's certificate on a notice of marriage| Additional District Registrar or District Registrar| Parties to marriage| 100.00| Cash  
5| Solemnizing marriage in Registrar’s office| Registrar| Parties to marriage| 750.00| Cash  
6| Solemnizing marriage in Additional District Registrar's or District Registrar's office| Additional District Registrar or District Registrar| Parties to marriage| 750.00| Cash  
7| Solemnizing marriage outside the Registrar’s office under section 38(1) or 38(2)| Registrar| Parties to marriage| 750.00| Cash  
Additional District Registrar or District Registrar| Parties to marriage|  50.00| Cash  
8| Solemnizing marriage outside the Additional District Registrar or District Registrar's office under section 38(1) or 38(2)| Additional District Registrar or District Registrar| Parties to marriage | 750.00| Cash  
Additional District Registrar or District Registrar| Parties to marriage|  50.00| Cash  
9| Obtaining a special license under section 27(3)| Government| Parties to marriage| 100.00| Cash  
10| Registration of marriage solemnized in registered place of public worship| District Registrar| Parties to marriage| 750.00| Cash

title: Marriage certificate from divisional secretariat
summary: Details for Marriage certificate from divisional secretariat.
description:
**Obtain Marriage certificate from divisional secretariat**

Application Form

  


Application Form Number and Name Description

  


Application for a Marriage Certificate (Sari and Osari) Examination of the Marriage Certificate Form 121 Marriage Certificate 

  


Notification of Marriage Form 100 If the place of marriage is a church or temple, submit a letter acceptable to the Registrar stating that if the marriage is to be held in accordance with one's religion, a letter from that place

  


Required Supporting Documents

•National Identity Card and two witnesses

•Marriage and Divorce _ Divorce Certificate, if applicable

•Death Certificate of the former spouse

  


Duration of the Process

  


Marriage notices should be submitted about an hour before the place of marriage.

  


Once the temple or church certifies that it is a legal marriage, the marriage certificate will be issued in 2 to 3 days.

  


•Submission Period

  


Step 1: Obtain a Marriage Notice Form

  


The Marriage Notice Form can be obtained from the Enquiry Office or the Marriage Secretary during any working hours.

  


Working Days: Monday to Friday

  


When the application is mailed, the time for the application to reach the relevant location is significantly increased.

  


Working Days: Monday to Friday

(Monday and Wednesday all day)

Window Opening Hours

9.00 a.m. to 12.30 p.m. and 1.00 p.m. to 3.00 p.m.

  


Holidays:_Any public and commercial holidays

  


Step 3: Obtaining Application Forms

  


Application forms can be obtained from the Enquiry Office or the Registered Divisional Secretariat Office on any working day.

  


Working days: Monday to Friday

  


Window opening hours: 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m./ 1:00 p.m. to 3:00 p.m.

  


Holidays: Any public and commercial holidays

  


Step 4: Submitting the application

  


When the application is mailed, it takes a lot of time to go to the relevant location. However, there is no time wasted when the application is submitted in person.

  


Working days: Monday to Friday

  


Window opening hours: 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. and 1:00 p.m. to 3:00 p.m.

  


Holidays: Any public and commercial holiday

  


Legal period

  


During life

  


Step by step procedure (Issue of marriage certificate)

  


Step 1: The applicant should obtain a marriage notice form from the Enquiry Office or the Registration Officer or the Divisional Secretary.

  


Step 2: The applicant should prepare and fill the notice form

  


Step 3: The applicant should obtain a letter of enquiry or an application form from the Registration Officer or the Divisional Secretary.

  


Step 4: The applicant should prepare and fill the application form

  


Step 5: The applicant should provide the necessary supporting information

  


Step 6: The application form can be sent to the Divisional Secretary by post or taken to the Divisional Secretary and handed over.

  


Step 7: The Divisional Secretary checks the application form and all the data received to be legal and sends the application form to the relevant temple or church.

  


Step 8: The applicant can obtain his marriage certificate by post or other means.

  


Note: If the applicant is found ineligible, the registration officer will send the application form back to the applicant along with the reason for the ineligibility.

  


Eligibility

  


If a Sri Lankan is getting married within the Divisional Secretariat of his/her province of residence, the marriage certificate can be issued by the relevant Divisional Secretariat Office.

  


Applicant must be above 18 years of age

  


Any applicant can obtain a marriage certificate. However, applicants

  


Note: If the applicant belongs to two Divisional Secretariat Divisions, the marriage should be performed at the Divisional Secretariat Office of their choice

  


Service Responsibilities Table

  


Additional District Registrar Births, Marriages and Deaths Registration Division.

  


Payments related to the service

  


Rs. 25/= per copy is charged. (From 3 copies and above)

  


Note: 

•If sending by stamp, send in a registered envelope

•If you need to send the certificate you have applied for by registered post, the registration duty should be included in the envelope.

title: Copy of Marriage certificate from divisional secretarit
summary: Details for Copy of Marriage certificate from divisional secretarit.
description:
**Obtain copy of Marriage certificate from divisional secretariat**

Issuance of a copy of a marriage certificate

  


Eligibility

  


•If any Sri Lankan gets married within the district limits and wants to obtain a copy of a marriage certificate legally, he should obtain it from the relevant Divisional Secretary of the area where the marriage took place.

  


•He should leave the area where the marriage took place and obtain the certificate from the relevant Divisional Secretary of the area where it is registered.

  


Application Form

  


Application Form Number Name Details

  


Application Form (General or Central) and / or Registration Search Form 121

  


Procedure for obtaining a copy of a marriage certificate

  


1Obtaining an application form

  


The applicant should obtain applications from the Enquiry Office or any Divisional Secretary of the relevant registration office.

  


The applicant must prepare and fill out the application.

  


The following supporting data must be provided

  


National Identity Card

  


Application Number

  


Year/Month

  


Page 121

  


2 Submission of the Application Form

  


The applicant may hand in the application form to the registering officer. Alternatively, a stamped envelope with the address indicated can be mailed

  


Note:_

  


•If the application form is mailed, it should be sent by registered mail.

  


•If the applicant knows the exact registration initials or the actual registration number, it is quicker and easier to obtain a copy.

  


•The marriage certificate will be mailed during the relevant period.

  


Application forms will be accepted from

  


9 a.m. to 12.30 p.m.

  


1 p.m. to 5 p.m. 4.45

  


Application Forms

  


Application Form Number Description

  


Application Form for a Copy of a Death Certificate or a Record Search Form B63 A

  


Obtaining a Copy of a Death Certificate

  


Obtaining a Copy of a Death Certificate

  


Timeline

  


Processing Time

  


If the applicant has provided the registration date or registration number of the original marriage certificate, the applicant can obtain a copy of the marriage certificate within 2 or 3 days

  


Note:_

  


If the required information cannot be provided, the application process may be delayed depending on the number of people involved

  


Time for Submission

  


Step 1Obtain a Marriage Application Form

  


The Marriage Application Form can be purchased from the Inquiry Office or the Marriage Secretary during any working hours 

  


Working days: Monday to Friday

  


(Monday and Wednesday all day)

  


Office hours: 9:00 AM to 12:30 PM and 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM

  


Holidays: Any public and commercial holidays

  


Step 2: Receiving the marriage application

  


There is no time wasted when submitting the application directly.

  


When the application is mailed, it takes time for the post office to reach the relevant location.

  


Working Days:_Monday to Friday

  


(Monday and Wednesday all day)

  


Window Opening Hours

  


9.00 AM to 12.30 PM and 1.00 PM to 3.00 PM

  


Holidays :_ Any public and commercial holidays

  


Legal Period

  


During Life

  


Step by Step Procedure (Issuance of a Copy of Marriage Certificate)

  


Step 1 The applicant should obtain a marriage application form from the Enquiry Section of the Divisional Secretariat Office or from the Registration Officer.

  


Step 2 The applicant should prepare and fill the application form

  


Step 3 The applicant should submit the application form by post or in person to the Registration Officer at the Divisional Secretariat

  


Step 4 The Divisional Secretary checks the application form received and all the data received are legal and records it in the record book.

  


Step 5 The applicant can collect his marriage certificate by post or in person.

  


Note:_

  


If the applicant is found ineligible, the Registration Officer will send the application form back to the applicant along with the reason for the ineligibility.

  


Special Cases

  


There are no special cases for this service

  


Cost of services

The stamp charges for obtaining the services are as follows

•A stamp charge of Rs. 25/= is charged per copy.

•If the date of registration is not known, it takes three months to search through the books and only one copy is issued after 3 months. Rs. 50/= should be paid as stamp duty.� Rs. 5/= should be paid each to obtain two copies.

•If the marriage is to be searched through the books within a period of 2 years, then stamp duty of Rs. 100/= should be paid.

summary: Details for Obtain a Certified Copy of the Certificate of Death.
description:
**Required Forms** [declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Declaration_of_a_Normal__sudden_Death.pdf)

**Registration of a Death occurred in a Rural Hospital**

  1. Refer the [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b63a.pdf) to the Divisional Secretariat of the area where the death is occurred.
  2. Application can be obtained from any Divisional Secretariat.
  3. Fees chargeable
  4. Where the date of registration or the no. of the entry is given the fee for one copy of the certificate is Rs. 100.00
  5. Where the date of registration or the no. of the entry is not given and a search of registers not exceeding two years is involved fee for one copy of the certificate is Rs. 200.00
  6. Required fee should pay to the Divisional Secretariat.
  7. Duly filled [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b63a.pdf) should submit to the Divisional Secretariat.
  8. If you like to obtain the certificate of death by post, submit a stamped envelop with the [application](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/b63a.pdf).
  9. If the death is registered a certificate of death will be send and if the death is not registered a letter to the effect will be send.

title: Registration of Death Through Divisional Secretariat
summary: Details for Registration of Death Through Divisional Secretariat.
description:
**Required Forms** [Form 63 A](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/DivisionalSecColo_death_certificate_b63a.pdf) [Form 15](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/DivisionalSecColo_death_certificate_b63a.pdf)

**Registration of Death Through Divisional Secretariat**

Issuance of a Death Certificate

  


Eligibility

  


Persons residing within the jurisdiction of the Divisional Secretary's Office may obtain a death certificate from the relevant Divisional Secretary's Office when a death occurs within that area.

  


Note:-

•If the place of death is outside the registered area, it is not necessary to obtain the certificate from the Divisional Secretary of the registered area.

•This information must be provided by a person who was with the person at the time of death or by a person who was last with him

•Failure to provide the above information is a punishable offence

  


Table of Service Responsibilities

  


Personal Information Name of the Department 

  


Birth, Marriage and Death Certificates Registration Location 

  


Birth, Marriage, Death Registration Division

  


Application Forms

  


Application Forms _ No., Name Details

  


Application Form for Obtaining a Death Certificate or Search Form for Records 

  


Registration of Death

  


Notification of Delay in Registration of Death

  


Registration after 3 Months of Death

  


Application for Registration of Death 

  


Deaths to be Provided for Registration of Death after Expiration of Time Statement of Details

  


Supporting Documents

  


The following supporting documents are required for death certification.

  


If the death is registered after 3 months, the following documents must be attached.

•Grama Niladhari certificate if the death occurred at home,

•Medical Niladhari certificate if the death occurred in a hospital

•Letter requesting a death certificate

•Death notice

•Two eyewitnesses with an affidavit

•A certificate from a priest who participated in the burial ceremony or a religious priest who participated in the burial ceremony

•If there is a funeral society, an acceptable letter from a position holder in it

•If it was a sudden death, the report of the coroner who came to investigate the death

  


Time limit

  


Time taken to prepare

  


The death certificate will be prepared and the applicant will receive it within 2 to 3 days if the Grama Niladhari or the hospital report is provided by the applicant.

  


Note:_If supporting letters and certificates are not provided with the application form, it may take up to a month.

  


Duration of submission

  


Step 1: A valid application form can be obtained from the Registrar's Office or the Enquiry Counter at the Divisional Secretariat during working hours.

  


Working days: Monday to Friday (Monday and Wednesday are suitable)

  


Window opening times: 9.00 a.m. to 12.00 p.m. and 1.00 p.m. to 3.00 p.m.

  


Holidays: All government commercial holidays

  


Step 2: Submission of application form

  


There is no time consuming process if you hand deliver the application form. If you post it, it will take some time to reach the relevant officer.

  


Working Days: Monday to Friday

  


(Monday and Wednesday)

  


Opening Hours: 9.00 am to 12.30 pm and 1.00 pm to 3.00 pm

  


Holidays: All Government and Commercial Holidays

  


Step by Step Procedure (Death Registration)

  


Step 1: Applicant submits a valid application form to the Registrar's Office or Enquiry Counter or to the Regional Secretary's Office.

  


Step 2: Applicant submits the completed application form.

  


Step 3: Applicant prepares supporting documents

  


Step 4: Applicant takes the application form to the Registrar's Office of the Divisional Secretariat and delivers it or mails it to the Divisional Secretariat.

  


Note: - The application form should be submitted with a stamped envelope

  


Step 5: Check the Grama Niladhari certificate and hospital report to see the authenticity of the application form and supporting documents received by the Divisional Secretary.

  


Step 6: The death certificate will be collected by hand or sent to the applicant by post.

  


Note: _If the applicant's application form is cancelled, the application form will be returned by post with the reason for the cancellation.

  


Sample Application Form with Sample Data

  


Application Form for Registration of Death

  


Death Details to be Provided for Registration of Death after Expiry of Time

  


Service Charges

•No need to apply for a general death. However, stamp duty is charged for the following events.

•A stamp duty of Rs. 20 is charged for registration of death within 3 months to 1 year.

•A stamp duty of Rs. 20 is charged for registration of death after 1 year.

title: Copy of death certificate from divisional secretaria
summary: Details for Copy of death certificate from divisional secretaria.
description:
**Obtain a copy of death certificate from divisional secretariat**

Obtaining a copy of a death certificate

  


Procedure to be submitted

  


1\. Obtaining a valid application form

  


The applicant should prepare a valid application form from the relevant Certificate Registrar or any Divisional Secretariat Office.

  


If 3 months have passed since the death, the following documents should be submitted.

  


•Grama Niladhari Certificate: If the death occurred at home

•Medical Officer Certificate: If the death occurred in a hospital

•The following data should be provided when registering after 3 months have passed since the death.

•Requested letter

•Death notice

•Names of 2 eyewitnesses

•Clergy or clergy participating in religious ceremonies (at the time of burial)

•If the funeral is conducted by a funeral home, funeral home, society or other office bearers,

•If the death occurred suddenly, it is inquired by the Sudden Deaths Section

  


Application Form Number - Place of Birth / Year / Month

  


General Registration Form - 63 A

  


In case of late registration - Form 15

  


2\. The application must be submitted by hand. The applicant can go to the Registrar's Office and hand over the application or mail it to the Registrar with a stamped envelope

  


3\. Note:_

  


If the application is sent by post, it should be sent by registered post.

  


Application hours

  


9.00 am to 12.30 pm

  


1.00 pm to 4.45 pm

  


The applicant should inform the Grama Niladhari immediately after the death, otherwise the Registrar should be informed within 5 days

  


The death certificate will be sent to the applicant by post

  


Method of submission

  


Step1: Obtaining applications

  


Application forms can be obtained from the Enquiry Section or the Registration Office during working hours.

  


Working Hours: -Monday, Tuesday (Monday and Wednesday all day)

  


Office Opening Hours: 9.00 a.m. to 12.30 p.m. 1.00 p.m. to 3.00 p.m.

  


Holidays: -All public and commercial holidays

  


Step2: Submission of Applications

  


Applications can be submitted directly to the relevant location without wasting time. However, when the applications are posted, it takes a long time to reach the relevant department.

  


Working Hours: Monday to Friday (Monday and Wednesday all day)

  


Office Opening Hours: 9.00 a.m. to 12.30 p.m. 1.00 p.m. to 3.00 p.m. Until 3.00

  


Holidays: Every public and commercial holiday

  


Duration

  


If a new death certificate is being prepared, the applicant should provide the date of the original death certificate within 2 or 3 days to facilitate retrieval of information. For example, it may take longer than usual to search for information that is not useful.

  


Step by Step Procedure (Issuance of a copy of the death certificate)

  


Step 1: The applicant can obtain a valid application form from the Registrar's Office Enquiry Counter or from the Regional Office.

  


Step 2: The applicant fills and prepares the application form

  


Step 3: The applicant prepares supporting documents

  


Step 4: Submit the application form to the Registrar's Office or submit it to the Divisional Secretary's Office

  


Note: - If the application is mailed, it should be mailed with a stamped envelope.

  


Step 5: The Divisional Secretary checks the application and supporting documents and verifies their authenticity

  


Step 6: The death certificate will be delivered to the applicant by hand or sent by post.

  


Note: - If the application is canceled, the reasons for cancellation The Registrar will send the application form by post.

  


Qualifications

  


In the event of the death of a Sri Lankan, a valid copy of the death certificate should be obtained from the Divisional Secretariat in charge of the division where the death occurred.

  


If the death occurred outside the home province, the Divisional Secretariat Office of the province where the death occurred should be informed.

  


Services and Responsibilities

  


Individual Information: Name of the deceased:

  


Registered Birth Certificate Date of Birth, Marriage and Death:

  


Registered Marriage Certificate and Death Certificate Place

  


Prices for the service

  


Stamp charges are as follows.

  


(1) If the date of registration or certificate number is available, the stamp fee is Rs. 25/= per copy. If more than two copies are obtained, Rs. 25/= each should be paid.

  


(2) If the date of registration is not known, it takes three months to find it through the books and it is issued One copy is made after 3 months. Rs. 50/= should be paid as stamp duty. To obtain two copies, Rs. 5/= should be paid each.

  


(3) If the search is to be made through books within a period of 2 years after the death, a stamp duty of Rs. 100/= should be paid.

description:
**Required Forms** _[declaration](https://gic.gov.lk/gic/pdf/Declaration_of_a_Normal__sudden_Death.pdf)_

**Registration of a Death Occurred at Home**

  1. Inform the Grama Niladari within 07 days of the death is occurred.
  2. Grama Niladari shall send a report to the registrar of the area.
  3. The death is registered by the registrar of births and deaths of the area where death has occurred. Declaration of death can be obtained from the registrar.
  4. Persons required to give information,
  5. i. Close relation present at the death.
  6. ii. Close relation who look after at the death.
  7. If there is no relation as given above.,
  8. iii. Relation who residing in the area of the registrar division where the death occurred.
  9. If there is no relation as given above.
  10. iv. Person present at the death
  11. v.Occupier where the death occurred.
  12. If there is no person as given above.
  13. vi.The person who cremate or buried the body.
  14. Death may be register free of charge within a period of 03 months. Death can be registered after 03 months. Please see the delayed registration of deaths.
  15. Certificate of death is issued to the informant free of charge.
